children.value: This page summarizes the mathematics behind

. The field-expansion
table is linear in the GG functions and their

-derivatives, which makes the
fit a (weighted) linear least-squares problem.

children.html: c∈{Bx,By,Bs}c \in \{B_x, B_y, B_s\}c∈{Bx​,By​,Bs​}

children.value:
B_c(x,y,z) = \sum_{(n,m)} CS_{c,b}(n,m; x,y)\, b_{(n,m)}(z)
           + \sum_{(n,m)} CS_{c,a}(n,m; x,y)\, a_{(n,m)}(z)
           + \sum_{m}     CS_{c,bs}(m; x,y)\, bs_{(m)}(z),
children.html: Bc(x,y,z)=∑(n,m)CSc,b(n,m;x,y) b(n,m)(z)+∑(n,m)CSc,a(n,m;x,y) a(n,m)(z)+∑mCSc,bs(m;x,y) bs(m)(z),B_c(x,y,z) = \sum_{(n,m)} CS_{c,b}(n,m; x,y)\, b_{(n,m)}(z) + \sum_{(n,m)} CS_{c,a}(n,m; x,y)\, a_{(n,m)}(z) + \sum_{m} CS_{c,bs}(m; x,y)\, bs_{(m)}(z),Bc​(x,y,z)=(n,m)∑​CSc,b​(n,m;x,y)b(n,m)​(z)+(n,m)∑​CSc,a​(n,m;x,y)a(n,m)​(z)+m∑​CSc,bs​(m;x,y)bs(m)​(z),

children.value: where each coefficient is a polynomial in the transverse coordinates,

children.html: CSc,f(n,m;x,y)=∑(coeff⋅gref k xp yq),CS_{c,f}(n,m; x,y) = \sum (\text{coeff}\cdot g_{\text{ref}}^{\,k}\, x^p\, y^q),CSc,f​(n,m;x,y)=∑(coeff⋅grefk​xpyq),
b(n,m)=dmbn/dzmb_{(n,m)} = \mathrm{d}^m b_n/\mathrm{d}z^mb(n,m)​=dmbn​/dzm
a(n,m)=dman/dzma_{(n,m)} = \mathrm{d}^m a_n/\mathrm{d}z^ma(n,m)​=dman​/dzm
bs(m)=dm+1a0/dzm+1bs_{(m)} = \mathrm{d}^{m+1} a_0/\mathrm{d}z^{m+1}bs(m)​=dm+1a0​/dzm+1

children.value:
is obtained by Taylor-extrapolating each
derivative,

children.html: f(n,m)(z0+dz)=∑j≥mdz j−m(j−m)! f(n,j)(z0),f_{(n,m)}(z_0 + \mathrm{d}z) = \sum_{j \ge m} \frac{\mathrm{d}z^{\,j-m}}{(j-m)!}\, f_{(n,j)}(z_0),f(n,m)​(z0​+dz)=j≥m∑​(j−m)!dzj−m​f(n,j)​(z0​),

children.value:
which makes the model linear in the base-plane unknowns. Each base plane is then
solved by weighted linear least squares over all field points lying within

accurate, so there is an
optimum.
For a base plane, the merit function minimized is

children.html: Merit=∑weight (Btable−BGG)2,\text{Merit} = \sum \text{weight}\,(B_{\text{table}} - B_{\text{GG}})^2 ,Merit=∑weight(Btable​−BGG​)2,

children.value: with a per-point weight that factors as

children.html: weight(x,y,dz)=wcore(x,y) wplane(dz)\text{weight}(x,y,\mathrm{d}z) = w_{\text{core}}(x,y)\, w_{\text{plane}}(\mathrm{d}z)weight(x,y,dz)=wcore​(x,y)wplane​(dz)

wcore(x,y)=core_weight rmax⁡2rmax⁡2+r2(core_weight−1),r2=x2+y2,w_{\text{core}}(x,y) = \texttt{core\_weight}\, \frac{r_{\max}^2}{r_{\max}^2 + r^2(\texttt{core\_weight}-1)}, \qquad r^2 = x^2 + y^2,wcore​(x,y)=core_weightrmax2​+r2(core_weight−1)rmax2​​,r2=x2+y2,

wplane(dz)=1+(outer_plane_weight−1) ∣dz∣dzmax⁡.w_{\text{plane}}(\mathrm{d}z) = 1 + (\texttt{outer\_plane\_weight}-1)\,\frac{|\mathrm{d}z|}{\mathrm{d}z_{\max}} .wplane​(dz)=1+(outer_plane_weight−1)dzmax​∣dz∣​.

children.value: (the default) weights all transverse points equally; a value

1 favours near-axis points, which is usually desirable since a beam spends most
of its time near the core.

weights all planes equally;
a value below 1 (but non-negative) down-weights the outer planes. When
